qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via remote_lookups-enabled URL expansion and hash lookup functions
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: While the finding targets the same unfurl project, it describes SSRF through outbound HTTP requests in parsers (parse_shortlink.py, parse_hash.py, parse_bluesky.py, parse_magnet.py). The advisory root cause is unbounded zlib decompression in parse_compressed.py:81-82. None of the finding's code locations include parse_compressed.py, and the root-cause mechanism (unvalidated HTTP requests vs. unbounded decompression) is fundamentally different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability: unbounded zlib decompression in unfurl/parsers/parse_compressed.py:81-82 (zlib.decompress(decoded) with no size limit). Finding 'ssrf-via-remote-lookups' targets the same unfurl project but describes SSRF through different parsers (parse_shortlink, parse_hash, parse_bluesky, parse_magnet) with a different root cause (unvalidated outbound HTTP requests). No finding references parse_compressed.py or the decompression bomb attack vector described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory identifies parse_compressed.py:81-82 as the vulnerable code location with zlib.decompress(decoded) and no max size parameter.

Finding 'ssrf-via-remote-lookups' references unfurl/app.py:91-96, parse_shortlink.py:62, parse_hash.py:36, parse_hash.py:46, parse_bluesky.py:71, parse_bluesky.py:91, parse_magnet.py:64 — none of these are parse_compressed.py.
Finding 'ssrf-via-remote-lookups' root cause is unvalidated outbound HTTP requests (SSRF), not unbounded zlib decompression (CWE-400/CWE-409).
Advisory PoC script references poc_decompression_bomb.py and compression bomb generation — no finding addresses this attack vector.
